_comment: Workflow templates. actor: ai = Claude does it · you = manual/green-light/fix · both = collaborative. Cyber Metric is computed client-side from step actors (higher = more automated).

workflows.name: DA ↔ CL — Design build
workflows.purpose: Turn an idea or spec into production-ready Figma screens.
workflows.when: Building or updating app/web screens for any brand.

steps.what: Generate the screen as JSX + design tokens
steps.how: DA designs from the spec + the brand's CLAUDE.md context
Approve or redirect BEFORE any build — the cheap gate that prevents rework
Deterministic handoff package the builder consumes

steps.what: Build native Figma frames with real auto-layout
steps.how: CL reads the bundle and builds via the Figma MCP
Visual check; spacing/token corrections — your main manual cost
You flag, AI fixes, repeat until clean

workflows.insight: Two human gates: direction (high value — kills rework early) and QA (your biggest manual cost). The middle is fully automated once the bundle contract holds.
suggestions: Add an AI self-QA step (list changed frames + flag off-token values) before your QA to shrink manual review.
Route the build step to Sonnet, not Opus — it's execution, not reasoning (see MODEL-ROUTING).
Template the spec intake so DA starts with less back-and-forth.

workflows.name: Magnific — Marketing asset
workflows.when: Posters, media kits, store previews, campaign assets.

steps.how: You set intent; AI fills gaps from brand context
AI generates via Magnific — or pulls from its non-AI stock library when a real photo fits
Pick a direction or request tweaks

workflows.insight: Your newest workflow. Highly automated except the creative green-light. Magnific's stock library lets you skip generation entirely when a real photo is better.
suggestions: Save winning layouts as reusable templates to skip the layout step next time.
Pre-define asset specs per channel so the brief becomes one line.
Once the Telegram bot ships, steps 5–6 run hands-off pending your gate.

workflows.name: UX Audit
workflows.purpose: Evaluate a product and produce prioritized, actionable fixes.
workflows.when: Recurring product reviews (e.g. Tino UX Audit).

steps.what: Cluster & rank issues by user impact

steps.how: Your only real input — confirm or reorder
AI files issues straight into your task board

workflows.insight: Nearly autonomous — your one input is validating priorities. Strong candidate to put on a recurring schedule.
suggestions: Schedule a recurring audit per active product.
Auto-file the resulting issues into the task board so they appear with heat scores.

newSuggestions.title: Marketing → lead approval loop
newSuggestions.idea: Wire the Magnific workflow into the Telegram bot so lead approvals + iterations run without you relaying.

newSuggestions.title: Audit → task board pipeline
newSuggestions.basis: Tino UX Audit is a hot recurring task
newSuggestions.idea: Make UX Audit a scheduled workflow that auto-creates heat-scored issues each cycle.

newSuggestions.title: Opus → Sonnet rebalancer
newSuggestions.idea: A pre-flight checklist that downshifts routine build/QA steps to Sonnet, raising every workflow's efficiency.
